sections.content:
The game is no longer over! Clicking the purple ring restored the game state. The white star is now at the purple ring position (18, 57), and the orange square is visible on the path. Let me check the current state and figure out the correct sequence.
Key findings from the game over:
1. Clicking the purple square causes GAME_OVER - DON'T DO THIS
2. The game can be restarted by clicking the purple ring after game over
Now I need to figure out the correct sequence to complete the level. Let me check the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 20:
        rs = [x for x,_ in n['boundary']]
        cs = [y for _,y in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rs)+max(rs))//2, (min(cs)+max(cs))//2)
        print(f"Square color={n['color']} center={center}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 20:
        rs = [x for x,_ in n['boundary']]
        cs = [y for _,y in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rs)+max(rs))//2, (min(cs)+max(cs))//2)
        children = [seg['nodes'][c]['color'] for c in n['children']] if n['children'] else []
        print(f"White px={n['pixels']} center={center} children={children}")

# Also check for orange target pixel
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        rs = [x for x,_ in n['boundary']]
        cs = [y for _,y in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rs)+max(rs))//2, (min(cs)+max(cs))//2)
        print(f"Orange px=1 center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
